title: Authentication API returning HTTP 500 displays a user-facing error message instead of silently failing or exposing a stack trace
description: Simulates a server-side failure (500 Internal Server Error) during the login API call and verifies the application handles it gracefully: shows a human-readable error to the user, does NOT expose internal details (stack traces, SQL errors, server paths), and does NOT leave the form in a broken state. Silent failures and stack-trace leaks are two of the most common high-severity bugs that reach production undetected.
expected_result: PASS: A friendly, non-technical error message is shown; the form remains usable; no stack trace, SQL, or file path is exposed in the UI or response body visible to the user; URL stays at /login. FAIL: Page shows a raw stack trace or database error, the UI freezes with a permanent spinner, the user is silently not logged in with no message, or — critically — the user is redirected to /dashboard despite the 500.

test_steps.action: Configure network interception to return HTTP 500 for the login API endpoint before navigating (done via test framework route mock)
test_steps.expected: Route interception is active; any POST to /api/auth/login will return 500

test_steps.action: Fill in the email field with a syntactically valid email
Fill in the password field with a plausible password
Click the submit button to trigger the login API call that will return 500
test_steps.expected: Form submits; a loading/spinner state appears momentarily

test_steps.action: Wait for the error state to resolve after the mocked 500 response
test_steps.expected: A user-facing error message is displayed, such as 'Something went wrong. Please try again later.'

test_steps.action: Verify the error message text does not contain server internals (stack trace keywords)
test_steps.expected: Error message does NOT contain text like 'NullPointerException', 'at com.', 'SELECT', 'ORA-', '/var/www/', or 'stack'

test_steps.action: Verify the user remains on the login page and has NOT been redirected
test_steps.expected: URL still shows /login; no redirect to /dashboard or /home occurred

test_steps.action: Verify the submit button is re-enabled and the form is still usable after the 500 error
test_steps.expected: Submit button is enabled; email and password fields are still accessible
